name: CSRF — No Token Protection
educational_objective: Understand the fundamental CSRF attack: a cross-origin POST request sent from an attacker page is automatically accompanied by the victim's cookies, allowing state-changing actions without the victim's knowledge.
scenario: BankPortal v1.0 — a basic online banking interface. The /transfer endpoint accepts POST requests with amount and recipient fields. No CSRF token is required. The browser automatically attaches the session cookie on any same-origin or cross-origin POST.
ar_scenario: بوابة BankPortal تقبل طلبات POST للتحويل بدون CSRF token. المتصفح يُرفق الكوكي تلقائياً مع أي طلب.

hints_metadata.content: The transfer endpoint doesn't check whether the request originated from the legitimate site. What does the browser send automatically with every request to a domain?
hints_metadata.ar_content: نقطة نهاية التحويل لا تتحقق من مصدر الطلب. ما الذي يُرسله المتصفح تلقائياً مع كل طلب للنطاق؟

hints_metadata.content: An attacker page on a different origin can submit a form that POSTs to BankPortal. The victim's browser will include their session cookie automatically — no JavaScript needed.
hints_metadata.ar_content: صفحة المهاجم يمكنها إرسال نموذج POST لـ BankPortal. متصفح الضحية سيُرفق الكوكي تلقائياً.

hints_metadata.content: Click 'Simulate Victim Click' — this triggers a cross-origin POST to /transfer. The server processes it as legitimate because the session cookie is present. That's CSRF.
hints_metadata.ar_content: اضغط 'Simulate Victim Click' — سيُطلق POST عبر origins. السيرفر يعتبره شرعياً لوجود الكوكي.

flag_validation.note: Returned from /simulate-victim endpoint on successful cross-origin POST exploit

hidden_solution_path.exploit_path: Attacker page → form POST /transfer amount=9999 recipient=attacker → victim browser auto-attaches session cookie → server processes transfer
hidden_solution_path.fix_summary: Add CSRF token to every state-changing form; validate token server-side; set SameSite=Strict on session cookies
